method_atom: A nonlinear classical field theory whose energy-minimizing configurations are topological solitons carrying a conserved integer charge. In the limit where the field is long-ranged (massless), the minimal-energy multi-soliton states are hollow shell-like structures with their density concentrated on the edges of an approximately spherical polyhedron. Introducing a finite-range (mass/screening) term destabilizes these extended shell configurations above a threshold charge, and numerical relaxation of the full nonlinear equations reveals a transition to flatter, more compact solutions that fragment into clusters of smaller-charge subunits.
object_atom: Skyrmions in the Skyrme model of nuclear physics: pion-field configurations whose topological (baryon) number represents atomic nuclei, studied with massless versus massive pions and compared against the structure of real nuclei.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is a topological-soliton field theory in which adding a finite-range mass term destabilizes hollow shell states and induces clustering into lower-charge subunits at high charge; the pions, baryon number, Skyrmions, and comparison to nuclei are the nuclear-physics-specific object.
